understanding.current_goal: Trace Zenith CSS determinism contract to implementation, specifically verifying whether compile_structured_with_source normalizes the id parameter to forward slashes for module_id and whether it enforces duplicate (module_id, order) uniqueness before returning CompilerOutput.style_blocks
understanding.latest_decision: Use topological sort for CSS: CSS blocks are ordered by dependency depth via compiler sort
understanding.next_action: Obtain zenith_compiler crate source (packages/compiler/src/script.rs and packages/compiler/src/compiler.rs) to verify module ID canonicalization and duplicate (module_id, order) enforcement inside the compiler

review.notes: Agent B's three completed findings are all supported by repository evidence: (1) LF normalization in compile_zen_source is confirmed at zenith_loader.rs — 'let source = source.replace("\r\n", "\n");' before compile_structured_with_source call. (2) resolve_id in zenith_loader.rs uses std::fs::canonicalize for relative imports (OS-specific separators), and compile_zen_source passes id directly to compile_structured_with_source without forward-slash normalization — confirmed. Additionally, collect_graph_style_blocks in bundle.rs also uses std::fs::canonicalize, reinforcing the module_id canonicalization gap on Windows. (3) The compiler source files (packages/compiler/src/script.rs, packages/compiler/src/compiler.rs) are indeed NOT listed in repository evidence, confirming the blocker. The stopping point is valid and accurately reflects the state. The next action (obtaining compiler source) is legitimate but cannot be completed within the constraint of only reading listed repository evidence — the agent correctly stopped rather than inventing conclusions about the compiler's internal behavior. No files were modified (files_changed is empty). No repeated work, no scope drift. The process_css function in utils.rs does NOT enforce duplicate (module_id, order) uniqueness — it relies on the compiler for that, which aligns with the contract's statement that 'The compiler ensures uniqueness of identity.' This enforcement point remains unverified due to missing compiler source.
